mechanism_narrative: A significant positive surprise in monthly non-farm payrolls growth signals accelerating economic momentum, leading to upward revisions in corporate earnings forecasts and driving positive equity returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:Payroll reports are a high-frequency indicator of economic health.
failure_condition: In periods following a trigger, the S&P 500's 20-day forward returns are not statistically different from periods without a trigger (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate policy (ZIRP) regime, an increase in inflation (CPI) lowers real interest rates, making equities more attractive relative to bonds and boosting their valuation. This effect holds as long as the Fed's policy rate is constrained at the lower bound.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, the correlation between CPI changes and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative or statistically insignificant over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales reflects robust consumer spending, a primary driver of corporate revenue. A positive 3-month change in retail sales predicts positive equity returns over the short term.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is ~70% of US GDP
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 3-month change in RSAFS and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is consistently below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth outpaces payroll employment growth, it signals an increase in labor productivity. This productivity boom expands corporate profit margins, leading to positive stock market returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year % change in INDPRO > Year-over-year % change in PAYEMS
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically significantly higher than when the condition is not met.

mechanism_narrative: A consistent decline in the unemployment rate over a 6-month period indicates a strengthening labor market and improving economic health. This positive momentum translates into higher expected corporate earnings and drives equity prices up over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 6-month change in UNRATE and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive or statistically insignificant over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts, a key leading indicator of economic activity, signals a looming slowdown in consumer spending and construction. A month-over-month decline greater than 10% predicts negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing is a highly cyclical, interest-rate sensitive sector
failure_condition: Following trigger events, the S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the labor market is tight (low unemployment) and inflation is accelerating, it signals an overheating economy. This combination forces the central bank to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and causing negative forward returns for equities.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.0% AND 3-month change in CPIAUCSL is positive
evidence_refs: fact:Fed's dual mandate on inflation and employment
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is positive or not statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, accelerating real GDP growth provides strong confirmation of economic healing. This reduces uncertainty and boosts earnings expectations, leading to positive equity returns. The signal is most powerful when emerging from a downturn.
evidence_refs: fact:Equity markets are forward-looking and price in economic recovery
failure_condition: In the first 3 years following a NBER-defined recession trough, periods of accelerating GDP growth are not followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns on average.
